title: We need to sort mounts so that one mount does not over mount another.
body:
Currenly we add mounts from images, volumes and internal.
We can accidently over mount an existing mount.  This patch sorts the mounts
to make sure a parent directory is always mounted before its content.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: It fixes the mount order but unmounting is now an issue: `device or resource busy`
@vrothberg Could you give me a command line where this causes an issue?

> @vrothberg Could you give me a command line where this causes an issue?

@rhatdan, see below:
```
podman run --rm -v `pwd`/install.md:/config/install.md uchiwa/uchiwa:latest  ls /config
```
@vrothberg, I am in a car with limited bandwith, could you check if the current patch set works for your use case.

> @vrothberg, I am in a car with limited bandwith, could you check if the current patch set works for your use case.

Don't hack and drive, sure thing. Unfortunately, I still see the error. The latest changes make a lot of sense though and I expected them to work. I'll dig a bit deeper.

@rhatdan Those mounts are stored in the run-time spec and it looks like we're not keeping that in memory. I am not very familiar with those parts of the code but I think we should keep the rspec as a field for the Container and use that instead `c.Config.Mounts`.

`Container.Config.Mounts` will only point to `[...]/userdata` at that point.
Do we even need `container.config.Mounts`? It's only ever used for holding the path to /dev/shm
Yes I think so, it keeps track of tmpfs mounted to be shared between containers.

It really doesn't belong in config, given it's not statically managed. And we're not doing any tracking to see if other containers are using our SHM before unmounting (we might want to look into doing that)

We should look into expanding the bind-mount handling we have in State now to include tmpfs creation (and mount options) and combine it with that. That should be a separate PR, though.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably ccbaa15) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@vrothberg Sadly took me a day to figure it out.  The issue you were seeing was caused by the Image Volumes being mounted as "shared" propagation.  Then when podman added the volume mount into the image volume the mount point showed up on the host mount namespace as well as in the container.  Changing volume images mounts to "private" fixes the problem.
@rhatdan Fix the debugging artifact and I'll merge
